summary: Zoom-as-default-provider feature fully tested. Backend API returns correct structure (zoom: mode='central', universal=true, connected=false; teams: mode='user', connected=true; meet: mode='user', connected=true). Frontend shows 4 provider options with correct badges: Zoom shows 'Configuration en cours' badge and 'Bientôt disponible' description when not configured, Teams shows 'Avancé' + 'Connecté', Meet shows 'Limité' + 'via Google Calendar', External shows 'Manuel'. Zoom card is disabled when not configured. NO REGRESSION: Teams and Meet appointments can still be selected and validated. Existing Teams appointment detail shows correct banners.

critical_code_review_comments: AppointmentWizard.js lines 565-608: Zoom provider card correctly implements conditional rendering based on isConfigured status
AppointmentWizard.js line 594: zoom-pending-badge correctly shows 'Configuration en cours' when not configured
AppointmentWizard.js line 599-603: Zoom description correctly shows different text based on configuration status
video_evidence_routes.py lines 277-312: provider-status endpoint correctly returns mode, universal, description for each provider
meeting_provider_service.py lines 70-102: Zoom create_meeting correctly sets join_before_host:true for central mode
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zoom-as-default-provider feature verified. Zoom is currently NOT configured (ZOOM_ACCOUNT_ID, ZOOM_CLIENT_ID, ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET not set in backend/.env), so it shows as disabled with 'Configuration en cours' badge. When Zoom credentials are configured, the zoom-recommended-badge should appear instead and the card should be enabled. Key test data: Teams appointment 82f9be58-c4b7-48fa-a2e4-ef3b0470e626 for banner verification. Provider status API returns mode='central' for Zoom and mode='user' for Teams/Meet.
